summary: Pavel Durov, founder of Telegram, discusses his lifelong fight for freedom of speech and user privacy against powerful governments, detailing Telegram's lean engineering, innovative features, and unique business model. He shares his stoic personal philosophy, including strict discipline, and recounts facing immense pressure, including an arrest in France and a poisoning attempt, all while emphasizing the importance of integrity and independent thought.

main_entities.name: Pavel Durov
main_entities.context: Founder and CEO of Telegram, central figure of the discussion on freedom, censorship, and technology.

main_entities.name: Telegram
main_entities.context: A messaging platform actively used by over 1 billion people, known for its focus on privacy, speed, and innovation.

main_entities.name: Freedom of Speech
main_entities.context: A core value Pavel Durov has spent his life fighting for, building tools to protect human communication.

main_entities.name: Censorship
main_entities.context: The act of suppressing communication, which Telegram actively resists from governments and organizations.

main_entities.name: Privacy
main_entities.context: The protection of user communication from surveillance, a fundamental principle of Telegram's design.

main_entities.name: VK (VKontakte)
main_entities.context: Pavel Durov's first social network, which he built and later had to relinquish due to government pressure.

main_entities.name: French Government
main_entities.context: Accused of arresting Pavel Durov and attempting to pressure Telegram into political censorship.

main_entities.name: TON (The Open Network)
main_entities.context: A blockchain technology initially developed by Telegram, now an open-source community project integrated into Telegram's ecosystem.

main_entities.name: Bitcoin
main_entities.context: Cryptocurrency that Pavel Durov invested in early and believes in as a fundamental means of exchange.

main_entities.name: Bureaucracy
main_entities.context: Discussed as a force that naturally accumulates power, suffocates free markets, and erodes individual freedoms.

main_entities.name: Discipline/Stoicism
main_entities.context: Pavel Durov's personal philosophy guiding his lifestyle, diet, exercise, and emotional resilience.

main_entities.name: AI-driven recommender systems
main_entities.context: Criticized for making people consume the same information, leading to a lack of competitive advantage and independent thought.

main_entities.name: ElevenLabs
main_entities.context: Podcast sponsor providing help with translation and dubbing for multi-language audio tracks.

main_entities.name: Apple
main_entities.context: Pressured Telegram to comply with Russian censorship demands in 2018 by blocking app updates.

main_entities.name: Edward Snowden
main_entities.context: His revelations about government surveillance influenced Telegram's focus on strong encryption and privacy.

key_details.fact: Telegram's core engineering team consists of approximately 40 people.
Pavel Durov owns 100% of Telegram, giving him full control over its principles and operations.
Telegram became profitable for the first time in 2024.
Pavel Durov takes a symbolic salary of one-third of a dollar from Telegram.
Pavel Durov performs 300 push-ups and 300 squats every morning as a self-discipline exercise.
Telegram has never shared a single private message with any government or intelligence agency.
Pavel Durov was arrested in France in August last year and spent almost four days in police custody.
Telegram Premium has over 15 million paid subscribers, generating significant recurring revenue.
Pavel Durov bought his first thousands of Bitcoin in 2013.
Pavel Durov believes he was poisoned in 2018, an event he had not publicly discussed before.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Nikolai Durov
other_persons_mentioned.context: Pavel's brother, a mathematician, programmer, and expert in cryptography, who co-founded VK and Telegram and helped with technical challenges.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Mark Zuckerberg
other_persons_mentioned.context: Founder of Facebook, whom Pavel met in Silicon Valley and whose platform VK competed with in its early days.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Elon Musk
other_persons_mentioned.context: Compared to Pavel Durov in leadership style, discussed for his emotionality, deep care for issues, and ability to run multiple ventures simultaneously.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Franz Kafka
other_persons_mentioned.context: 20th-century writer whose works, particularly 'The Trial,' are discussed by Lex Fridman for their eerie parallels to bureaucratic absurdity and the suffocation of the human spirit.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Max Brod
other_persons_mentioned.context: Kafka's friend who refused to burn his manuscripts, preserving his literary work.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Gregor Samsa
other_persons_mentioned.context: Fictional character from Kafka's 'The Metamorphosis,' used to illustrate feelings of helplessness and dehumanization.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Albert Camus
other_persons_mentioned.context: Author of 'The Plague,' mentioned in the context of dystopian literature.

other_persons_mentioned.name: John B. Calhoun
other_persons_mentioned.context: Ethologist who conducted the 'mouse paradise experiment' (Universe 25), discussed for its insights into abundance and societal collapse.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Stalin
other_persons_mentioned.context: Mentioned as an example of an authoritarian regime that used propaganda to justify limiting freedoms.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Mao
other_persons_mentioned.context: Mentioned as an example of an authoritarian regime that used propaganda to justify limiting freedoms.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Navalny
other_persons_mentioned.context: Russian opposition leader whose groups the Russian government demanded VK take down in 2011.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Snoop Dogg
other_persons_mentioned.context: Celebrity whose blockchain-based 'gifts' were launched on Telegram, demonstrating a new form of digital collectibles.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Steve Jobs
other_persons_mentioned.context: Co-founder of Apple, mentioned for his philosophy on hiring 'A players' and the importance of design and craftsmanship.

other_persons_mentioned.name: Jony Ive
other_persons_mentioned.context: Former Chief Design Officer at Apple, mentioned alongside Steve Jobs for their dedication to design and user experience.

other_persons_mentioned.name: President Macron
other_persons_mentioned.context: French President, whose team's initial reaction to certain footage was mentioned in the context of government narratives and misinformation.
